 {"id":"bd-1","title":"Phase 1: Project Setup","description":"**EPIC: Project Foundation & Repository Structure**

## Goal
Establish the foundational file structure, versioning, and CI/CD scaffolding that all subsequent work depends on.

## Rationale
A well-organized project structure from day one prevents technical debt accumulation. The giil project demonstrated that a clean, minimal structure with clear separation of concerns makes maintenance and contribution easier. This phase creates the skeleton that gives the project its identity.

## Key Decisions Encoded
- VERSION file as single source of truth (not package.json, not embedded in script)
- MIT License for maximum adoption
- Examples directory for user guidance WITHOUT polluting user's XDG config
- No je_*.txt files in repo (those were development artifacts from original sync script)

## Success Criteria
- Repository has all foundational files in place
- CI can at least do basic syntax validation
- A contributor can clone and understand the structure immediately","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 setup completed: VERSION, LICENSE, .gitignore exist. Remaining items (bd-104, bd-105, bd-106) can be done in parallel.","labels":["foundation","setup"]}
 {"id":"bd-10","title":"Phase 10: Subcommand Implementations","description":"**EPIC: All Subcommand Implementations**

## Goal
Implement all subcommands: sync, status, init, add, list, doctor, self-update, config.

## Rationale
The subcommand architecture provides clear separation of concerns and discoverable CLI UX. Each command has a single responsibility and can be developed/tested independently.

## Command Overview
- sync: The main event - clone missing repos, pull existing ones
- status: Read-only view of repo states (useful before sync)
- init: First-run setup, creates config dirs and files
- add: Add a repo to the appropriate list file
- list: Show configured repos (with filters)
- doctor: System health check (deps, config, auth)
- self-update: Update ru itself from GitHub releases
- config: View/modify configuration values

## Main Processing Loop (sync/status)
The core loop processes repos sequentially (parallel is v2), tracking results in NDJSON, displaying progress, and handling errors without stopping.

## Success Criteria
- All commands work correctly
- Help text is accurate for each command
- Exit codes are correct per command","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:49.601795412-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:49.601795412-05:00","close_reason":"Core subcommands implemented: sync, status, init, add, list, config","labels":["commands","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.276445145-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.309247652-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.339321557-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.367035466-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-10","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.396960711-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-308","title":"Auto-init on first run","description":"**Automatically initialize config on first sync**

## What
When user runs `ru sync` with no config, automatically initialize instead of erroring.

## Why
Forcing `ru init` as a separate step is poor UX. Modern CLI tools should 'just work' on first run.

## Behavior
```bash
$ ru sync
First run detected. Initializing configuration...
  Created ~/.config/ru/config
  Created ~/.config/ru/repos.d/repos.txt

No repositories configured yet.
Add repos with: ru add owner/repo

$ echo 'But wait, what if they pass a URL directly?'

$ ru sync https://github.com/owner/repo
First run detected. Initializing configuration...
  Created ~/.config/ru/config  
  Created ~/.config/ru/repos.d/repos.txt

Adding and syncing: owner/repo
  Cloned: owner/repo
```

## Implementation
In cmd_sync(), before processing:
```bash
if [[ ! -d "$RU_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    log_info "First run detected. Initializing configuration..."
    ensure_config_exists
    # If args provided, treat as ad-hoc sync
    # If no args, explain how to add repos
fi
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- First sync auto-initializes
- If URL/repo passed, add and sync it
- If no args, explain next steps
- `ru init` still works for explicit initialization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:23.339456108-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:23.339456108-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented auto-init in cmd_sync() at lines 1129-1185.  {"id":"bd-309","title":"Simplify to single repos.txt by default","description":"**Use single repos.txt file instead of public/private split**

## What
Change from public.txt + private.txt to single repos.txt file.

## Why
The public/private split adds complexity without clear benefit:
- gh handles auth automatically
- Most users don't need the separation
- Simpler mental model = better UX

## Design
```
~/.config/ru/repos.d/
└── repos.txt          # All repos, public and private
```

If users want organization, they can add comments:
```
# My projects
owner/repo1
owner/repo2

# Work projects (private)
company/internal-tool
company/secret-project
```

## Migration
If old public.txt/private.txt exist:
1. Merge into repos.txt
2. Keep originals as backup
3. Log migration message

## ru add Changes
- Remove --private flag (no longer needed)
- Just add to repos.txt

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single repos.txt is default
- Old format auto-migrates
- `ru add` simplified
- Comments work for organization","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:00.352604542-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:00.352604542-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - using single repos.txt by default (line 497)","labels":["config","simplicity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-309","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.678924519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-309","depends_on_id":"bd-305","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.70835933-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-310","title":"Change default PROJECTS_DIR to ~/projects","description":"**Use ~/projects as default instead of /data/projects**

## What
Change DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR from /data/projects to ~/projects.

## Why
/data/projects is very specific and unusual:
- Most users don't have /data
- It's not a standard path
- ~/projects is more universally appropriate

## Implementation
```bash
# Old
DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="/data/projects"

# New
DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="$HOME/projects"
```

## First Run
On first run, if ~/projects doesn't exist, ask:
```
Where should repositories be cloned?
  [1] ~/projects (will be created)
  [2] Current directory: /path/to/cwd
  [3] Custom path
```

Or just default to ~/projects and create it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default is ~/projects
- Works on macOS and Linux
- Creates directory if needed

 {"id":"bd-4","title":"Phase 4: Logging System","description":"**EPIC: Structured Logging & Output System**

## Goal
Implement a logging system that maintains strict stream separation: stderr for human-readable output, stdout for structured data only.

## Rationale
This is an automation-grade tool. When someone runs `ru sync --json | jq .`, they need clean JSON on stdout without progress messages mixed in. Conversely, interactive users need beautiful, informative progress output. Stream separation makes both possible.

## Stream Rules
- stderr: log_info, log_warn, log_error, log_step, log_success, banners, summaries
- stdout: JSON output (--json mode), repo paths (default mode)

## Per-Repo Logging
Each repo's git output goes to a dated log file: ~/.local/state/ru/logs/YYYY-MM-DD/repos/owner_repo.log. This allows debugging without cluttering terminal output.

## NDJSON Result Tracking
During runs, we write newline-delimited JSON records to a temp file. This allows aggregation for summary reports without holding everything in memory.

## Success Criteria
- Running `ru sync 2>/dev/null` produces only structured output
- Running `ru sync --json 2>&1` shows progress on stderr, JSON on stdout
- Per-repo logs are written and accessible","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:49.632565691-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:49.632565691-05:00","close_reason":"Logging functions implemented in Phase 2 skeleton","labels":["logging","output"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4","depends_on_id":"bd-2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.049523389-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-401","title":"Implement log_info(), log_warn(), log_error()","description":"**Implement basic logging functions**

## What
Core logging functions for info, warning, and error messages.

## Why
Consistent logging with proper coloring and stream targeting improves UX and debuggability.

## Stream Rule: ALL go to stderr
Human-readable output ALWAYS goes to stderr. This keeps stdout clean for structured data.

## Implementation
```bash
log_info() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return
    echo -e "${BLUE}info:${NC} $*" >&2
}

log_warn() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}warn:${NC} $*" >&2
}

log_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}error:${NC} $*" >&2
}
```

## Quiet Mode
log_info respects QUIET_MODE. log_warn and log_error always print (errors should never be silenced).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All output goes to stderr
- Colors applied correctly
- Quiet mode silences info only","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:21:42.75602124-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:21:42.75602124-05:00","close_reason":"log_info(), log_warn(), log_error() already implemented in ru at lines 181-197","labels":["logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-401","depends_on_id":"bd-4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.622072877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-401","depends_on_id":"bd-204","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.650551877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-401","depends_on_id":"bd-205","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.679551689-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-402","title":"Implement log_step(), log_success(), log_debug()","description":"**Implement progress and debug logging**

## What
Functions for step progress, success indicators, and debug output.

## Why
Progress indication keeps users informed. Debug output aids troubleshooting.

## Implementation
```bash
log_step() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return
    echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} $*" >&2
}

log_success() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $*" >&2
}

log_debug() {
    [[ "$VERBOSE_MODE" != "true" ]] && return
    echo -e "${DIM}debug:${NC} $*" >&2
}
```

## Verbose Mode
log_debug only prints when VERBOSE_MODE is true. Useful for troubleshooting without cluttering normal output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Step shows arrow prefix
- Success shows checkmark
- Debug only shows in verbose mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:21:43.783410072-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:21:43.783410072-05:00","close_reason":"log_step(), log_success(), log_verbose() already implemented in ru at lines 199-207","labels":["logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-402","depends_on_id":"bd-401","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.708428388-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-404","title":"Implement get_repo_log_path()","description":"**Generate per-repo log file paths**

## What
Function to generate consistent log paths for each repository.

## Why
Per-repo logs allow detailed debugging without cluttering terminal. Users can review git output after the fact.

## Path Structure
```
~/.local/state/ru/logs/
├── 2026-01-03/
│   ├── run.log
│   └── repos/
│       ├── github.com_owner_repo1.log
│       └── github.com_owner_repo2.log
└── latest -> 2026-01-03
```

## Implementation
```bash
get_repo_log_path() {
    local repo_name="$1"
    local date_dir
    date_dir=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
    # Replace / with _ for filesystem safety
    echo "${RU_LOG_DIR}/${date_dir}/repos/${repo_name//\//_}.log"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Paths are deterministic
- Date-organized
- Safe for filesystem (no slashes in filename)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:25:13.765790853-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:25:13.765790853-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented get_repo_log_path(), get_run_log_path(), and update_latest_symlink() functions","labels":["logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-404","depends_on_id":"bd-202","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.768614221-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-405","title":"Implement write_result()","description":"**Write NDJSON result records**

## What
Function to append a result record to the temp results file.

## Why
We accumulate results during processing for later aggregation. NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) is easy to parse and append.

## Implementation
```bash
write_result() {
    local repo="$1"
    local action="$2"
    local status="$3"
    local duration="${4:-}"
    local error="${5:-}"

    # Escape for JSON
    error="${error//\"/\\\"}"
    error="${error//$'\n'/\\n}"

    cat >> "$RESULTS_FILE" << EOF
{"repo":"$repo","action":"$action","status":"$status","duration":${duration:-null},"error":"$error","timestamp":"$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"}
EOF
}
```

## Result Fields
- repo: Repository identifier
- action: clone|pull|skip
- status: ok|current|updated|failed|diverged|conflict|dry_run
- duration: Seconds (null if not applicable)
- error: Error message (empty string if none)
- timestamp: ISO 8601 UTC

## Acceptance Criteria
- Valid JSON per line
- Proper escaping of special characters
- Appends to temp file","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:38.353268001-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:18:38.353268001-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in ru script","labels":["json","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-405","depends_on_id":"bd-206","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.800661474-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-502","title":"Implement gum_confirm()","description":"**Yes/No confirmation with fallback**

## What
Function for yes/no prompts using gum confirm with fallback to read.

## Why
We need confirmations for destructive operations and optional installations. Must work with or without gum.

## Implementation
```bash
gum_confirm() {
    local prompt="$1"
    local default="${2:-n}"  # Default to No for safety
    
    if ! can_prompt; then
        # Non-interactive: return based on default
        [[ "$default" == "y" ]]
        return
    fi
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        gum confirm "$prompt"
    else
        local response
        local yn="y/N"
        [[ "$default" == "y" ]] && yn="Y/n"
        echo -n "$prompt [$yn]: " >&2
        read -r response
        response="${response:-$default}"
        [[ "${response,,}" == "y" || "${response,,}" == "yes" ]]
    fi
}
```

 {"id":"bd-608","title":"Make gh CLI optional for public repos","description":"**Fall back to git clone for public repos**

## What
Make gh CLI optional when only syncing public repositories.

## Why
Many users only sync public repos. Requiring gh installation for this is unnecessary friction. The original plan over-relied on gh.

## Design Change
1. Try `git clone https://github.com/owner/repo` first for HTTPS URLs
2. If that fails with auth error, THEN check for gh
3. Only require gh if:
   - User has private repos in their list
   - Public clone fails with auth error
   - User explicitly wants gh features

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    local url="$1"
    local target_dir="$2"
    
    # Try plain git clone first (works for public repos)
    if output=$(git clone --quiet "$url" "$target_dir" 2>&1); then
        log_success "Cloned: $repo_name"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # If auth error, try gh
    if [[ "$output" =~ (Authentication|403|401) ]] && check_gh_installed && check_gh_authenticated; then
        local clone_target
        clone_target=$(url_to_clone_target "$url")
        if gh repo clone "$clone_target" "$target_dir" -- --quiet 2>&1; then
            log_success "Cloned (via gh): $repo_name"
            return 0
        fi
    fi
    
    log_error "Clone failed: $repo_name"
    return 1
}
```

## ensure_dependencies() Change
Don't fail if gh is missing. Instead:
- Warn that private repos won't work
- Continue for public repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- Public repos clone without gh
- Private repos work with gh
- Clear messaging about what requires gh
- `ru doctor` shows gh status as optional","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:50.845755683-05:00","close_reason":"Enhancement deferred: current implementation requires gh but can be enhanced later to try plain git clone first for public repos","labels":["deps","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.558501167-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-608","depends_on_id":"bd-602","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.590518915-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-701","title":"Implement parse_repo_url()","description":"**Parse all GitHub URL formats**

## What
Function to extract host, owner, and repo from various URL formats.

## Why
Users will provide URLs in many formats. We must handle them all correctly.

## Supported Formats
1. https://github.com/owner/repo
2. https://github.com/owner/repo.git
3. git@github.com:owner/repo.git
4. github.com/owner/repo
5. owner/repo (assumes github.com)

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_url() {
    local url="$1"
    local -n _host=$2
    local -n _owner=$3
    local -n _repo=$4

    # Normalize: strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"

    if [[ "$url" =~ ^git@([^:]+):(.+)/(.+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^https?://([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    elif [[ "$url" =~ ^([^/]+)/([^/]+)$ ]]; then
        _host="github.com"
        _owner="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _repo="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Using nameref
We use bash nameref (-n) to return multiple values. Requires Bash 4.3+.

 {"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:56:12.946839267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented sanitize_path_segment() with comprehensive character replacement and safety checks","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8","title":"Phase 8: Git Operations","description":"**EPIC: Git Clone/Pull Operations with Plumbing**

## Goal
Implement git operations using plumbing commands for reliable status detection, avoiding string parsing of porcelain output.

## Rationale
Parsing 'Already up to date' is fragile - it varies by git version and locale. Git plumbing commands give deterministic, parseable output. We use `git rev-list --left-right --count` for ahead/behind, `git status --porcelain` for dirty detection.

## Key Patterns
- Always use `git -C "$repo_path"` instead of cd
- Capture output with `if output=$(git ... 2>&1); then ... else exit_code=$?; fi`
- Check for divergence by comparing old_head vs new_head after pull
- Support multiple strategies: ff-only (safe default), rebase, merge

## Remote Mismatch Detection
If a local repo exists but origin URL differs from expected, we warn and skip. This prevents accidentally overwriting unrelated repos.

## Success Criteria
- Clone operations work with proper error handling
- Pull operations detect all status conditions (current, updated, diverged, conflict)
- No string parsing of user-facing git messages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. ## What
Function to verify a directory is a valid git repository.

## Why
Before pulling, we need to confirm the directory is actually a git repo, not just a regular directory with the same name.

```bash

    [[ -d "$dir/.git" ]] || git -C "$dir" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null
}
```

 {"id":"bd-802","title":"Implement get_repo_status()","description":"**Get repository status using git plumbing**

## What
Function to determine repo status (current, ahead, behind, diverged, dirty) using plumbing commands.

## Why
Parsing 'Already up to date' is fragile (varies by git version and locale). Plumbing commands give deterministic output.

## Implementation
```bash
get_repo_status() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local do_fetch="${2:-false}"

    if [[ ! -d "$repo_path/.git" ]]; then
        echo "STATUS=not_git AHEAD=0 BEHIND=0 DIRTY=false BRANCH="
        return
    fi

    # Fetch if requested
    if [[ "$do_fetch" == "true" ]]; then
        git -C "$repo_path" fetch --quiet 2>/dev/null || true
    fi

    # Check dirty status
    local dirty="false"
    if [[ -n $(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null) ]]; then
        dirty="true"
    fi

    # Get current branch
    local branch
    branch=$(git -C "$repo_path" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    # Check for upstream
    if ! git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse --verify '@{u}' &>/dev/null; then
        echo "STATUS=no_upstream AHEAD=0 BEHIND=0 DIRTY=$dirty BRANCH=$branch"
        return
    fi

    # Get ahead/behind using plumbing
    local ahead=0 behind=0
    read -r ahead behind < <(git -C "$repo_path" rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{u} 2>/dev/null || echo "0 0")

    # Determine status
    local status
    if [[ "$ahead" -eq 0 && "$behind" -eq 0 ]]; then
        status="current"
    elif [[ "$ahead" -eq 0 && "$behind" -gt 0 ]]; then
        status="behind"
    elif [[ "$ahead" -gt 0 && "$behind" -eq 0 ]]; then
        status="ahead"
    else
        status="diverged"
    fi

    echo "STATUS=$status AHEAD=$ahead BEHIND=$behind DIRTY=$dirty BRANCH=$branch"
}
```

```bash
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"

    fi
```

## What
Function to load repos from a list file, skipping comments and blank lines.

## Why
List files are the primary way users specify repos. We need robust parsing.

## List Format
```
# Comment lines
# Blank lines ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@branch
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Implementation
```bash
load_repo_list() {
    local file="$1"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
        return 0  # Empty list, not an error
    fi
    
    while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
        # Skip empty lines and comments
        [[ -z "$line" || "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
        
        # Trim whitespace
        line="${line#"${line%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
        line="${line%"${line##*[![:space:]]}"}"
        
        echo "$line"
    done < "$file"
}
```

## What
Function to parse `repo@branch` and `repo as custom-name` syntax.

## Why
Users need to pin branches and customize local directory names.

## Syntax Patterns
- `owner/repo` -> repo, default branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo@develop` -> repo, develop branch, repo as name
- `owner/repo as myname` -> repo, default branch, myname as name
- `owner/repo@develop as myname` -> repo, develop branch, myname as name

## Implementation
```bash
parse_repo_spec() {
    local spec="$1"
    local -n _url=$2
    local -n _branch=$3
    local -n _local_name=$4
    
    # Extract 'as <name>' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)[[:space:]]+as[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]]; then
        spec="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _local_name="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _local_name=""
    fi
    
    # Extract '@branch' if present
    if [[ "$spec" =~ ^(.+)@([^@]+)$ ]]; then
        _url="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
        _branch="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
    else
        _url="$spec"
        _branch=""
    fi
}
```

Multiple list files or formats might reference the same repo. We process each path only once.

```bash
dedupe_repos() {
    # Input: lines of repo specs
    # Output: unique repo specs by resolved path
    
    local -A seen_paths
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        if [[ -n "$local_name" ]]; then
            path="$PROJECTS_DIR/$local_name"
        else
            path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        fi
        
        if [[ -z "${seen_paths[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            seen_paths[$path]=1
            echo "$spec"
        else
            log_debug "Skipping duplicate: $spec (same path as previous)"
        fi
    done
}
```

 {"id":"bd-904","title":"Implement detect_collisions()","description":"**Warn about path collisions**

## What
Function to detect when different repos would resolve to the same local path.

## Why
With flat layout, org1/utils and org2/utils collide. Users need to know before we overwrite.

## Difference from dedupe
- dedupe: Same repo referenced twice (ok, skip second)
- collision: Different repos mapping to same path (warning!)

## Implementation
```bash
detect_collisions() {
    local -A path_to_url
    local collisions=0
    
    while IFS= read -r spec; do
        local url branch local_name
        parse_repo_spec "$spec" url branch local_name
        
        local path
        path=$(url_to_local_path "$url" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT")
        
        local norm_url
        norm_url=$(normalize_url "$url")
        
        if [[ -n "${path_to_url[$path]:-}" ]]; then
            local existing="${path_to_url[$path]}"
            if [[ "$existing" != "$norm_url" ]]; then
                log_warn "Collision detected: $path"
                log_warn "  URL 1: $existing"
                log_warn "  URL 2: $norm_url"
                ((collisions++))
            fi
        else
            path_to_url[$path]="$norm_url"
        fi
    done
    
    return $collisions
}
```

+        log_warn "Could not verify checksum (checksums.txt not found or incomplete)"
+        if can_prompt; then
+            if ! gum_confirm "Continue without checksum verification?"; then
+                log_info "Update cancelled"
+                exit 0
+            fi
+        else
+            log_error "Cannot proceed without checksum verification in non-interactive mode"
+            exit 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Verify the downloaded script is valid bash
+    if ! bash -n "$temp_dir/ru" 2>/dev/null; then
+        log_error "Downloaded file is not valid bash script"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    # Get the path to the current script
+    local script_path
+    script_path=$(realpath "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")
+
+    # Check if we can write to the script location
+    if [[ ! -w "$script_path" ]]; then
+        log_error "Cannot write to $script_path (permission denied)"
+        log_info "Try: sudo ru self-update"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    # Atomic replacement: copy to temp in same directory, then mv
+    local script_dir
+    script_dir=$(dirname "$script_path")
+    local temp_script="$script_dir/.ru.update.$$"
+
+    log_step "Installing update..."
+    cp "$temp_dir/ru" "$temp_script"
+    chmod +x "$temp_script"
+
+    # Atomic move to replace
+    if mv "$temp_script" "$script_path"; then
+        log_success "Updated to v$latest_version"
+        echo "" >&2
+        log_info "Run 'ru --version' to verify"
+    else
+        rm -f "$temp_script"
+        log_error "Failed to install update"
+        exit 1
+    fi
 }
